title: T015D2 survivor: volume_pct_threshold=0.60 ~= regime median (decorative gate) — tighten before materialization
why: Loop A survivor 20260527-XAUUSD-BEAR_QUI-T015D2 (phase5_custom CUSUM+volume short) passed pre-flight with PROCEED_WITH_CAVEAT, but the DA flagged (medium) that volume_pct_threshold=0.60 sits at the regime volume median (q=0.6185) — it selects the upper ~half, not genuine expansion, so the gate may be decorative (the same arithmetic that was HIGH in T015 pre-reframe). Also: orthogonality was argued vs roc_63 (slow trend) but the relevant check is volume vs contemporaneous CUSUM-breach magnitude. Before B0040 materialization: raise threshold to ~0.75 (and recheck n_trades>=50 tension), and have the lookahead reviewer measure rank-corr(volume, |breach return|). UPDATE 2026-05-27: module materialized as pipeline/primaries_phase5/phase5_t015d2.py and adversarially lookahead-reviewed (verdict PROCEED_WITH_CAVEAT, NO high-severity lookahead — prefix-stability test proves causality). The threshold concern was NOT a lookahead bug but an EDGE-ATTRIBUTION risk. DA-mandated diagnostic to run at promote/discard: (a) fraction of cond_cusum_down events surviving the volume>=0.60 gate; (b) rank-corr(volume_rank, abs(log-return)) on breach bars in BEAR_QUIET. If gate removes <~15% of breaches OR corr>0.7, the volume filter is decorative and the survivor is effectively a bare CUSUM-down primary — re-attribute edge before promoting.
value_rationale: The first pre-flight survivor; tightening the gate determines whether it has real edge or is a median-cut artifact before the slow M3 audit.

history.reason: Lookahead review cleared; recording edge-attribution diagnostic from code-commit DA review
